reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly encodes the informal statement. The `squarish` predicate captures "n is a perfect square OR the distance from n to the nearest perfect square is a perfect square": the ∀ v : ℕ clause pins down w² as a minimizer (ℕ-squares cover all integer squares via w² = |w|²), and IsSquare |n - w²| says this minimum distance is a perfect square. S correctly counts squarish integers in [1,N]. The predicate p correctly states α,β > 0 with S(N)/N^α → β via Tendsto...atTop (𝓝 β) on ℤ, with (N:ℝ)^α using Real.rpow. The final disjunction "(3/4,4/3) is the unique p-solution, OR no solution exists" matches "find α,β or show none exist" — uniqueness of α in the asymptotic sense is mathematically automatic so no spurious strengthening. Verification against the informal examples (6 and 7 not squarish, 2 squarish) confirms the encoding works correctly.
